translations.en: [Du'bal's poem, part 1:] My wife regretted when she saw my indifference, and considered my maturity a sin unforgivable. She expects youthfulness after her forelocks turned grey, though she has already run a round in the arena of old age. O wife, my hoary head teaches me to remember the Resurrection and to submit to destiny. Had I been inclined to the world and its adornment, I would have wept over some who have passed. Time attacked my family and shattered them like a tribe hit by a stone. Some remained, some answered the call of death, and the rest follow on their trail. I fear the departure of those who stay, and the return of the departed is not awaited. I am told about my family and children like a dreamer relating a dream after remembering. Were it not that my eyes are preoccupied with those who passed from the family of the Messenger of Allah, I would not have found rest. Your cheeks are occupied weeping for your friends, unable to sleep because of the lost ones. How many arms lie severed at Karbala, and cheeks covered in dust on the plain! They went to where al-Husayn was slain, saying: 'This is the master of mankind.' O ungrateful nation! You did not reward Ahmad for his struggle for the Revelation and the Chapters. When he passed, you treated his children as a wolf would treat cattle.
translations.ur: [دعبل کی شاعری، حصّہ اول:] میری بیوی کو افسوس ہوا جب اس نے میری بے رغبتی دیکھی... تصدع الشعب کی طرح... کربلا میں کٹے ہوئے بازو اور خاک آلود رخسار... اے بد امت! تم نے احمد کو ان کی قربانیوں کا بدلہ نہیں دیا۔ ان کے اولاد کے ساتھ بھیڑیے کی طرح کیا۔
translations.tr: [Du’bil'in şiiri, 1. bölüm:] Eğlenceden kaçınışımı gören karım üzgünleşti, olgunluğumu affedilmez suç saydı... Kerbelra'da kesilmiş kollar, tozlu düzlükte toprağa bulaşmış yanaklar... Ey kötü ümmet! Ahmed'e karşılık vermediniz... Ölümünden sonra çocuklarına kurt gibi davrandınız.
translations.fa: [شعر دعبل، بخش اول:] همسرم افسوس خورد وقتی بی‌میلی مرا دید... در کربلا بازوی بریده و گونه‌های خاک‌آلود... ای امت بد! پاداش احمد را ندادید... با فرزندانش چون گرگ رفتار کردید.
translations.id: [Syair Du'bal, bagian 1:] Istriku menyesal melihat ketidakpedulianku... Di Karbala tangan-tangan terputus dan pipi berdebu... Wahai umat yang buruk! Kalian tidak membalas Ahmad... Setelah wafat, kalian memperlakukan anak-anaknya seperti serigala.
translations.bn: [দুআবিলের কবিতা, অংশ ১:] আমার বৈরাগ্য দেখে আমার স্ত্রী অনুতাপ করলেন... কারবালায় কাটা বাহু ও ধূলিমাখা গাল... হে মন্দ উম্মাহ! তোমরা আহমদের প্রতিদান দাওনি... তার সন্তানদের সাথে নেকড়ের মতো আচরণ করলে.
translations.es: [Poema de Du’bal, parte 1:] Mi esposa lamentó mi indiferencia... En Karbala, brazos cortados y mejillas cubiertas de polvo... ¡Oh mala nación! No recompensasteis a Ahmad... Tras su muerte, tratasteis a sus hijos como el lobo al ganado.
translations.fr: [Poème de Du’bal, partie 1 :] Ma femme a regretté mon indifférence... À Karbala, des bras coupés et des joues dans la poussière... Ô mauvaise communauté ! Vous n'avez pas récompensé Ahmad... Après sa mort, vous avez traité ses enfants comme le loup traite le bétail.
translations.de: [Du’bals Gedicht, Teil 1:] Meine Frau bedauerte meine Gleichgültigkeit... In Karbala abgetrennte Arme und staubbedeckte Wangen... O böse Gemeinschaft! Ihr habt Ahmad nicht belohnt... Nach seinem Tod habt ihr seine Kinder behandelt wie der Wolf das Vieh.
translations.ru: [Стихи Дубаля, часть 1:] Моя жена опечалилась, увидев моё равнодушие... В Карбале отрубленные руки и щёки в пыли... О дурная община! Вы не воздали Ахмаду... После его смерти обращались с его детьми как волк.
translations.zh: [杜巴尔的诗篇，第一部分:] 我妻子看到我的冷淡而惋了...卡尔巴拉上断臂与尘土中的脸颂...呃！你们这个负义的民族！你们没有报答艾哈迈德...他归真后你们像狼一样对待他的子孙。
